source.name: Collabora Online local third-party documentation snapshot
source.license_or_consent: Primarily MPLv2 per third_party/collabora-online/README.md and README.FILENOTICES.md; fixture uses structural profile data only.

profile_summary: A large collaborative document-editing project with root docs, contributor policy, developer notes, docker/build guides, security notes, accessibility notes, and test documentation.

folder_taxonomy: Root README for project overview and integration guidance
docker/ for build and deployment recipes
test/ for test data and test guidance
Root policy files for contributing, security, and file notices

note_type_mix.concept: Architecture and integration concepts appear in README and dev notes.
note_type_mix.project: Developer notes track specific implementation surfaces such as cursor following, locale, keyboard shortcuts, and socket ownership.
note_type_mix.reference: File notices, security, test, and build notes define operational contracts.
note_type_mix.tutorial: Build, docker, and integration sections describe repeatable workflows.

structure.approximate_note_length: A long project README plus compact topic notes and policy references.
tag_patterns: No Obsidian-style tags in source docs; folder names and headings act as tags.
Developer note filenames provide topic labels such as accessibility, debug, locale, keyboard, and sockets.
wikilink_patterns: Markdown links connect root docs to external docs, SDK docs, and build references.
Developer notes often mention related subsystems without complete reciprocal links.
backlink_patterns: Root README acts as a hub, while dev-note backlinks are sparse.
Policy and build docs form implicit clusters through repeated subsystem names.
stale_contradictory_duplicated_patterns: README integration guidance overlaps with build and docker guides.
Developer notes can lag behind current subsystem behavior.
Policy docs and templates duplicate contribution requirements in different forms.
data_exclusion_boundary: Public project docs contain no local user vault notes.
Benchmark uses structure only and does not copy sensitive deployment configuration.
No issue metadata, credentials, or local integration endpoints are committed.
task_pattern_examples: Find the authoritative policy when guidance is duplicated across root docs and templates.
Compare a developer note with a broader README statement.
Summarize build or integration constraints with source paths.

workflow_templates: Find the authoritative setup note among repeated public docs.
Connect sparse developer notes to project-level concepts.
Preserve provenance when creating generated wiki summaries from external docs.
fixture_influence: Generated wiki pages with source_paths frontmatter.
Scope-boundary tasks around public docs versus restricted local configuration.

known_exclusions: No provider request or response payloads
